original_abstract: This review explores a class of molecules characterized by chemical bonds in which a bare group-14 atom (C–Pb) coordinates to σ-donor ligands (L) or transition metal fragments [TM] via donor-acceptor interactions. Utilizing modern quantum chemical methodologies, we provide a comprehensive analysis of the underlying electronic structures and bonding characteristics of these species.
humanized_abstract: This review focuses on a new class of molecules, where the bonding involves purely non-covalent interactions, such as between a bare group-14 atom (C–Pb) and σ-donor ligands (L) or transition metal fragments [TM]. Modern quantum chemical methods are employed to gain detailed insight into the electronic structure and bonding in these fascinating molecules.

Modern quantum chemical methods are employed to gain detailed insight into the electronic structure and bonding in these fascinating molecules.
